item.title: Ukrainian, Romanian jailed in UK over Starmer arson plot ordered by mystery Russian
item.description: A British court sent two men to prison on Friday for setting fire to property linked to Prime Minister Keir Starmer in a plot orchestrated by a mysterious Russian-speaking figure. Ukrainian national Roman Lavrynovych, 22, and Stanislav Carpiuc, a 27-year-old Romanian citizen, were jailed for seven years and two years, respectively, after being found guilty of conspiracy to damage property by fire. The men targeted a car and two properties linked to Starmer over three nights in May 2025 on the...

item.title: Iran will not receive any money — Trump
item.description: The US leader claimed that Tehran met Washington "out of desperation"

item.title: Top Dutch diplomat advises Kiev against strikes on civilian infrastructure
item.description: According to Tom Berendsen, the Dutch side is in continuous contact with Ukraine, conveying concerns about civilian facilities being hit

item.title: Belarus reserves right to act against Ukraine to protect its citizens
item.description: "In the Republic of Belarus, we will take all necessary measures to protect the lives and ensure the safety of our citizens," Igor Nazaruk said

item.title: Russia, Iran agree to bring Russian specialists back to Bushehr NPP
item.description: On June 17, Rosatom Director General Alexey Likhachev stated that the Bushehr NPP project in Iran remains a priority for the company

item.title: Israeli army reports striking Hezbollah targets in southern Lebanon
item.description: According to the report, at least 16 people were killed in Israeli airstrikes on Hezbollah strongholds in the Nabatieh Governorate in southern Lebanon

item.title: Kiev seeks Brazilian president to act as mediator in talks with Russia — media
item.description: Brazilian officials believe Kiev is guided by the fact that, unlike European politicians, Lula da Silva maintains a trust-based relationship with Russian President Vladimir Putin

item.title: Fighting flares in Lebanon: 18 die in strikes, Israel says soldiers killed
item.description: Lebanon said 18 people were killed on Friday in Israeli air strikes in the south, while Israel’s military reported its first soldiers killed there since the sealing of a US-Iran deal to halt the Middle East war, including in Lebanon. Israel had said it was striking Hezbollah targets overnight and into the morning, while the Iran-backed militant group said it was attacking Israeli forces around the southern town of Nabatieh. “The intensive Israeli air strikes carried out from midnight until this...

item.title: Death toll from Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon climbs to 23
item.description: The localities of Kfar Sir, Harouf, Ad-Doueir, and Charqiyeh were bombed, with casualties among women and children

item.title: IN BRIEF: What is known about cancelation of planned US-Iran talks in Swiss resort
item.description: US Vice President JD Vance may have postponed his trip to Switzerland for the signing of a memorandum of understanding between the United States and Iran because of Tehran’s demands regarding Lebanon
